message: 🌱 Add caddy: Prometheus metrics are redundant and slow mission (#469)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "caddy-4644-prometheus-metrics-are-redundant-and-slow",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "caddy: Prometheus metrics are redundant and slow",
+    "description": "Prometheus metrics are redundant and slow. This issue affects 10+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify caddy troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your caddy deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=caddy\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors related to: Prometheus metrics are redundant and slow"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Check current resource configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant caddy resources:\n```bash\nkubectl get resource -A\nkubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>\n```\nWhile digging into Caddy's source code I've noticed that every (!) route handler is wrapped in a `metricsInstrumentedHandler` which updates Prometheus metrics during request execution. While it is a g"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Prometheus metrics are redundant and slow",
+        "description": "Worth noting that `metrics` is already a handler directive to serve the actual metrics exposition endpoint (see https://caddyserver.com/docs/caddyfile/directives/metrics), so unfortunately I think we'll need a different config for it. We could have a `labels` directive maybe:\n\n```\nlabels [<field> <v\n```yaml\nmetrics {\r\n\tlabel host {http.request.host}\r\n\tlabel content_type {http.response.header.Content-Type}\r\n\tlabel static_label \"same value for everyone!\"\r\n}\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Prometheus metrics are redundant and slow is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The root cause is: Worth noting that `metrics` is already a handler directive to serve the actual metrics exposition endpoint (see https://caddyserver.com/docs/caddyfile/directives/metrics), so unfortunately I think we'll need a different config for it. We could have a `labels` directive maybe:\n\n```\nlabels [<field> <value>] {\n\t<field> <value>\n}\n```\n\nI'm thinking we might want to support somekind of setup where you c. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "metrics {\r\n\tlabel host {http.request.host}\r\n\tlabel content_type {http.response.header.Content-Type}\r\n\tlabel static_label \"same value for everyone!\"\r\n}",
+        "labels [<field> <value>] {\r\n\t<field> <value>\r\n}",
+        "metrics {\r\n  labels {\r\n    host {http.server.host}\r\n  }\r\n  include {\r\n    reverse_proxy {\r\n      upstream {http.handlers.reverse_proxy.upstream}\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n  exclude {\r\n    request_size_bytes\r\n  }\r\n}"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
